key_fields.subjective: Referred for evaluation of recurrent headaches and cognitive complaints. Patient reports daily headaches for 6 months, bifrontal, pressure-like, rated 5-7/10. Associated photophobia and nausea. Also reports difficulty concentrating, word-finding difficulty, and short-term memory lapses since a motor vehicle accident 8 months ago. Pre-accident: no headache history.
key_fields.objective: Alert, oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Motor 5/5 all extremities. Sensation intact. Cerebellar function normal. Romberg negative. Mini-Mental Status Exam 28/30 (lost points on delayed recall and serial 7s).
key_fields.imaging_findings: MRI brain with contrast (reviewed): No acute intracranial pathology. Small nonspecific white matter hyperintensities in bilateral frontal lobes, possibly post-traumatic. No mass or midline shift.
key_fields.diagnoses: Post-traumatic headache syndrome; Post-concussion syndrome with cognitive impairment; Migraine without aura.
key_fields.medications: Topiramate 25mg daily, titrate to 50mg BID. Sumatriptan 50mg PRN for acute migraine.
key_fields.plan: Neuropsychological testing recommended to quantify cognitive deficits. Headache diary for 4 weeks. MRA if headaches do not improve. Return 6 weeks.

key_fields.subjective: Comprehensive neuropsychological evaluation for post-concussion cognitive complaints. Patient reports persistent difficulty with attention, memory, and executive function since MVA. Denies pre-accident cognitive issues. Currently not working. Lives alone, manages basic ADLs independently.
key_fields.objective: Testing administered over 4 hours: WAIS-IV, WMS-IV, Trail Making A&B, Stroop Color-Word, Wisconsin Card Sorting Test, Boston Naming Test, CVLT-II, Rey Complex Figure, Conners CPT-3, MMPI-2-RF, TOMM. Results: Processing speed: low average (SS 85); Attention/concentration: borderline (SS 78); Verbal memory: low average (SS 82); Visual memory: average (SS 95); Executive function: low average (SS 84); Language: average (SS 98). Effort measures (TOMM): passed both trials, valid profile. MMPI-2-RF: Elevated somatic complaints and low positive emotions scales; no evidence of over-reporting.
key_fields.diagnoses: Mild neurocognitive disorder due to traumatic brain injury; Post-concussion syndrome.
key_fields.plan: Results consistent with mild neurocognitive disorder secondary to TBI. Recommend cognitive rehabilitation therapy 2x/week. Vocational rehabilitation assessment. Accommodations for return-to-work: reduced screen time, written task lists, quiet workspace. Re-evaluate in 6 months.

key_fields.subjective: Follow-up after neuropsych testing. Headache frequency reduced from daily to 3-4 per week with topiramate. Cognitive symptoms persist but slightly improved with cognitive rehab. Reports tingling in fingers bilaterally, a known topiramate side effect.
key_fields.objective: CN II-XII intact. Motor 5/5. Decreased sensation to pinprick distal fingertips bilaterally. MMSE 29/30.
key_fields.diagnoses: Post-traumatic headache, improving; Post-concussion cognitive impairment; Topiramate-induced paresthesias.
key_fields.medications: Reduce topiramate to 25mg BID (from 50mg BID) due to paresthesias. Continue sumatriptan PRN. Add magnesium glycinate 400mg daily for migraine prophylaxis.
key_fields.plan: Continue cognitive rehabilitation. Monitor paresthesias after dose reduction. If headaches worsen, consider switching to propranolol. Follow-up 8 weeks.

key_fields.subjective: Initial PT evaluation for cervicalgia and post-concussion vestibular symptoms. Patient reports neck stiffness and dizziness with quick head movements since MVA. Has been doing cognitive rehab separately.
key_fields.objective: Cervical ROM: Flexion 35/50 degrees; Extension 40/60; Rotation R 55/80, L 60/80; Lateral flexion R 30/45, L 35/45. Cervical tenderness bilateral upper trapezius and suboccipital muscles. Dix-Hallpike: negative bilaterally. BPPV screen negative. Mild dizziness with rapid horizontal head turns. Balance: single leg stance 15 sec R, 12 sec L (normative >30 sec). Dynamic Gait Index: 20/24 (mild impairment).
key_fields.diagnoses: Cervicalgia; Cervicogenic dizziness; Post-concussion vestibular dysfunction.
key_fields.plan: Plan: 2x/week for 8 weeks. Vestibular rehabilitation exercises, cervical stretching and strengthening, gaze stabilization exercises, balance training.
key_fields.mss: Cervical ROM limited all planes; Flexion 35/50; Extension 40/60; Rotation R 55/80, L 60/80; Lateral flexion R 30/45, L 35/45. DGI 20/24.

key_fields.subjective: Headaches now 1-2 per week. Paresthesias resolved after topiramate dose reduction. Dizziness much improved with vestibular PT. Cognitive function subjectively better. Plans to attempt part-time work next month.
key_fields.objective: CN II-XII intact. Motor 5/5. Sensation normal. Cervical ROM improved. No nystagmus.
key_fields.diagnoses: Post-traumatic headache, significantly improved; Post-concussion syndrome, improving; Cervicogenic dizziness, resolved.
key_fields.plan: Continue current medication regimen. Taper topiramate if headaches remain controlled. Support return to part-time work with accommodations. Follow-up in 3 months.

key_fields.subjective: Fitness for duty evaluation for return to part-time office work. Patient was data analyst prior to MVA. Reports improved headaches and cognition. Concerned about screen tolerance and multitasking capacity.
key_fields.objective: Physical exam unremarkable. Neurological exam normal. Functional capacity assessment: Can sit for 2 hours continuously before requiring 10 min break. Sustained attention adequate for simple tasks, decreased with complex multi-step tasks. Screen tolerance 90 min before onset of headache.
key_fields.diagnoses: Post-concussion syndrome, recovering; Fitness for modified duty.
key_fields.plan: Cleared for part-time work (4 hours/day) with restrictions: 1) Frequent screen breaks every 60-90 min, 2) Written task instructions, 3) Quiet work environment, 4) No driving for work purposes. Re-evaluate in 8 weeks for progression to full duty.

key_fields.subjective: Working part-time for 6 weeks. Headaches infrequent, about 1 per week, mild intensity. No dizziness. Cognition adequate for current workload but struggles with longer meetings. Tolerating topiramate 25mg BID well.
key_fields.diagnoses: Post-traumatic headache disorder, near resolution; Post-concussion cognitive impairment, mild residual.
key_fields.plan: Begin tapering topiramate: 25mg daily x2 weeks, then 25mg every other day x2 weeks, then discontinue. Continue magnesium. Occupational medicine follow-up for full duty evaluation. Consider repeat neuropsych testing in 6 months to document recovery.
key_fields.medications: Topiramate taper as above. Continue magnesium glycinate 400mg daily. Sumatriptan 50mg PRN.

key_fields.subjective: Re-evaluation for progression to full-time duty. Working part-time 4 hours/day for 4 months without issues. Headaches rare (2-3 per month). Off topiramate for 4 weeks. No dizziness or cognitive complaints at current workload.
key_fields.objective: Exam normal. Functional capacity: Can sit for 4+ hours with standard breaks. Screen tolerance >3 hours. Sustained and divided attention within normal limits on screening assessment.
key_fields.diagnoses: Post-concussion syndrome, nearly resolved; Cleared for full-time duty with minimal restrictions.
key_fields.plan: Cleared for full-time work (8 hours/day). Minimal restrictions: ergonomic workstation setup recommended, screen breaks every 2 hours. No restriction on driving. Final follow-up with neurology recommended to close case.
